data.title: China Merchants Bank Focuses on Maintaining Leading Net Interest Margin
data.description: China Merchants Bank has highlighted the impact of the low-interest-rate environment on traditional banking profitability. According to RTHK, Chairman Miao Jianmin emphasized the importance of maintaining a leading net interest margin in the industry during this year's outlook at the bank's performance briefing. President and CEO Wang Liang noted that the bank's net interest margin remained ahead of peers last year and expects a stable yet slightly reduced margin this year, with a narrower decline compared to last year. He anticipates further interest rate cuts and reserve requirement reductions, which could affect loan yields. Wang explained that overall credit demand remains insufficient, with intense competition among banks leading to disorderly competition. Vice President Peng Jiawen stated that the goal for this year is to minimize the reduction in the interest margin and achieve stabilization as soon as possible, while maintaining industry leadership in net interest margin.

data.title: Hong Kong's Potential as a New Economic Financing Hub Highlighted
data.description:
Hong Kong's Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Christopher Hui, has expressed optimism about Hong Kong's potential to become a new economic financing center. According to RTHK, Hui noted that mainland enterprises already possess competitiveness overseas and are expected to use Hong Kong as a financing platform for internationalization.

In an interview with a local newspaper, Hui mentioned that with the launch of the Hong Kong Gold Settlement service within the year, the spot market will be ready. He suggested that the existing gold futures contracts at the Hong Kong Stock Exchange might need adjustments or the introduction of new products to align with market developments.

Hui revealed that over 10 domestic and international banks and industry players have joined the Hong Kong Gold Settlement initiative, which will conduct over-the-counter settlement operations in collaboration with warehouses. He did not rule out the possibility of expanding the settlement service to include other metals in the future. With the establishment of the refining company MKS PAMP in Hong Kong, Hui hopes to attract more gold merchants to the region.

The Secretary emphasized the importance of expanding the market for Hong Kong Gold Settlement, targeting promotion in Southeast Asian countries where gold is often considered an asset option. He mentioned that cooperation could extend to government levels, such as transactions related to national gold reserves, and further into private market commercial participation. Hui expressed the desire to clearly outline the support and convenience Hong Kong can offer, with the possibility of settling over-the-counter gold contracts in Hong Kong.

Hui also noted that as of the end of January, the number of London Metal Exchange (LME) recognized warehouses in Hong Kong had increased to 15, storing up to 23,000 tons of bulk commodities. Efforts will be made to encourage gold merchants and the bulk commodity industry to utilize more of Hong Kong's services.

data.title: MYX Finance Scheduled for Token Unlock on April 6
data.description: MYX Finance is set to unlock approximately 9.72 million tokens on April 6 at 0:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this information comes from Web3 asset data platform RootData's token unlock data. The unlocked tokens are valued at around $2.36 million.

data.title: Anthropic's AI Claude Takes Over Code Development
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). Anthropic is advancing its AI capabilities with Claude, which is now responsible for designing its next iteration. Engineers at Anthropic have shifted from writing code themselves to overseeing Claude's work, indicating a significant change in their development process. The company's CEO highlighted the rapid pace of AI evolution, expressing uncertainty about the time left before the cycle completes. This development underscores the swift advancements in AI technology.

data.title: Vietnam's Financial Potential Highlighted in New York Meeting
data.description:
On March 30, Pendle co-founder @tn_pendle met with representatives from Deutsche Bank, Morgan Stanley, BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, and Anchorage Digital in New York to discuss the development of Vietnam's international financial center. According to BlockBeats, the meeting included discussions with Vietnam's Deputy Prime Minister.

During the talks, Pendle's co-founder emphasized Vietnam's potential in the tokenized bonds, ETFs, and private credit markets. He also highlighted the country's substantial talent pool, advocating for its capabilities in these financial sectors.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Surpasses $70 per Ounce with 0.41% Daily Increase
data.description: Spot silver prices have surged past the $70 per ounce mark, experiencing a 0.41% increase within the day. According to Jin10, this rise in silver prices reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment towards precious metals. The increase highlights the current trends in the commodities market, where silver is gaining traction among investors. This development comes amid broader market movements and economic indicators influencing precious metal valuations.

data.title: South Korea Considers Expanding Driving Restrictions Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: South Korea is contemplating extending driving restrictions to the general public if oil prices exceed $120 per barrel, according to the country's finance minister. Bloomberg posted on X that this measure aims to mitigate the effects of the ongoing conflict in Iran on South Korea's energy supplies. The government is closely monitoring the situation and preparing contingency plans to address potential disruptions in oil imports. The finance minister emphasized the importance of ensuring energy security and minimizing economic impact during this period of uncertainty.

data.title: Trader Opens $3.38 Million Short Position on Brent Crude Oil
data.description: A trader named Loracle has initiated a $3.38 million short position on Brent crude oil with 20x leverage, according to Foresight News. This move occurred two hours ago. Concurrently, Loracle has begun closing their long position on HYPE.

data.title: Abraxas Capital Increases Short Positions in WTI and Brent Crude Oil
data.description:
Abraxas Capital has significantly increased its short positions in WTI and Brent crude oil, according to Hyperinsight monitoring. According to BlockBeats, over the weekend, the firm added more than $44 million to its positions across two addresses, with average prices of $102 for WTIOIL and $108 for BRENTOIL. This marks a 48% increase in short positions compared to Friday.

Following this round of additions, each address now holds over $17 million in WTIOIL shorts and over $50 million in BRENTOIL shorts, bringing the total position to $136 million. Due to the continuous rise in oil prices, these positions have shifted from profit to a combined unrealized loss of $7.2 million.

data.title: In the NBA regular season game, the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the New York Knicks, and the Thunder's top two profitable accounts earned a combined profit of over $70,000.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, the outcome of the "NBA Regular Season Knicks vs. Thunder" prediction on Polymarket has been confirmed: the Thunder won the game. The top-ranked account, Dishonest-Bloom, profited approximately $50,000 from the Thunder's winning position, while the second-ranked account, S-Works, profited approximately $21,000, for a combined profit exceeding $70,000. Odaily Seer previously reported that in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Thunder vs. Knicks" prediction event, an account with over $2.2 million in profits (address: 0xee00ba338c59557141789b127927a55f5cc5cea1) purchased $100,000 worth of bets on the Thunder defeating the Knicks at an opening price of 77¢. The Thunder won this NBA regular season game and are currently ranked 1st in the Western Conference, while the Knicks are ranked 3rd in the Eastern Conference. Odaily Seer continuously focuses on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Trump: If Iran charges tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, the US will take swift action.
data.description: When asked about Iran's plan to collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, US President Trump said, "I'd have to confirm if that's true, but the United States can very quickly stop this. We can do that in two minutes. We can do it so fast you'll get dizzy." He stated that Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, and Bahrain are retaliating against Iran. "I was very surprised that they were attacked. But once attacked, their retaliation was very good. We have very strong communication, but people from all these countries say they've been fighting." (Jin Shi)

data.title: RBI Supports Rupee Amid Foreign Stock Outflows in India
data.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has taken measures to support the rupee as foreign investors continue to withdraw from Indian stocks. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the persistent trend of foreign outflows affecting the stock market. Despite these challenges, local funds have maintained their purchasing activity, providing some stability to the market. The RBI's intervention aims to mitigate the impact of these outflows and stabilize the currency. This situation reflects ongoing economic dynamics in India, where domestic investors play a crucial role in counterbalancing foreign investment trends.

data.title: Robert Kiyosaki Warns of Inflation Risks Amid Rising National Debt
data.description:
Robert Kiyosaki, author of 'Rich Dad Poor Dad,' recently expressed concerns on social media about the ongoing expansion of national debt and increased money supply, which he believes will drive inflation upward. According to Odaily, Kiyosaki warned that U.S. dollar savings face continuous depreciation pressure.

He also highlighted the potential for prolonged geopolitical conflicts to support rising oil prices, further exacerbating the inflationary environment. In light of the current global debt, currency, and inflation scenario, Kiyosaki emphasized the importance of personal financial awareness and asset allocation.

Kiyosaki holds a favorable view of assets such as gold, silver, oil, food, Bitcoin, and Ethereum.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum See Modest Gains as AI Sector Leads Market
data.description: The cryptocurrency market experienced slight gains over the past 24 hours, with the AI sector showing the most significant increase at 2.35%. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin's value rose by 0.41%, surpassing $66,000, while Ethereum saw a 0.54% increase, climbing above $2,000. These movements reflect a modest upward trend in the market.

data.title: Gate internally supports the use of Vibe Coding by all employees, accelerating AI-driven efficiency upgrades.
data.description: According to Gate CBO Kevin Lee X, Gate has fully supported the Vibe Coding tool internally, and employees can use it after completing an application. The current system is centered around Cursor Enterprise, covering multiple mainstream large models such as Claude and GPT, and is flexibly invoked through a unified AI gateway to meet diverse needs in R&D and daily work. Kevin Lee stated that AI is becoming a key infrastructure driving organizational efficiency and innovation. Gate has increased its investment in AI tools and model resources, currently investing millions of dollars monthly. The company encourages all employees to actively embrace AI, improve work efficiency through Vibe Coding, and deeply integrate AI into development processes and daily scenarios to further optimize execution and output capabilities. In the future, Gate will continue to optimize AI tools and resource allocation, expand more application scenarios, and accelerate the implementation of AI-driven efficiency upgrades throughout the company.

data.title: The author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad" believes that inflation and geopolitical conflicts will drive up oil prices, and is bullish on assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum.
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki, author of *Rich Dad Poor Dad*, posted on social media that the continued expansion of national debt and the increase in money supply will drive inflation upwards, putting sustained downward pressure on dollar savings. He also believes that geopolitical conflicts may persist for a long time, providing upward support for oil prices and further exacerbating the inflationary environment. Kiyosaki points out that in the current context of global debt, currency, and inflation, personal financial literacy and asset allocation are particularly crucial, and he holds a relatively optimistic view on assets such as gold, silver, oil, food, and Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: AI TRENDS | All Co-Founders Depart from Elon Musk's AI Startup xAI
data.description: Ross Nordeen, a key figure in Elon Musk's AI startup xAI, has left the company, marking the departure of all 11 co-founders. According to PANews, Nordeen, who was 36 years old, worked closely with Musk, coordinating priorities and driving the execution of various company tasks. The departure follows the merger of xAI with SpaceX in early February, which led to a significant exit of co-founders. Earlier this month, Musk commented that xAI was initially not structured correctly and is undergoing a foundational rebuild.

data.title: Expert Warns of Re-centralization in Yield Restrictions
data.description: Markus Thielen has expressed concerns over recent restrictions, describing them as a "clear re-centralization of yield." According to NS3.AI, Thielen's statement highlights the potential shift in the decentralized finance landscape, where yield opportunities may become more centralized. This development could impact investors seeking decentralized yield options, as the market adjusts to these new constraints.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Analyst Warns of Prolonged Middle East Conflict Impact on Asian Currencies
data.description:
According to Jin10, Lloyd Chan, a senior currency analyst at Mitsubishi UFJ Bank, has indicated in a research report that recent developments suggest the Middle East conflict may become prolonged. Chan noted that risk aversion, coupled with high energy prices, could elevate U.S. inflation and maintain U.S. interest rates at high levels for an extended period, thereby supporting the U.S. dollar and increasing resistance for risk-sensitive assets.

Chan highlighted that Asian currencies such as the Indian rupee, Philippine peso, South Korean won, and Thai baht are particularly vulnerable due to their reliance on imported energy and sensitivity to high oil prices through inflation and current account channels. Data from the London Stock Exchange Group shows that the U.S. dollar rose by 0.5% against the Philippine peso, reaching 60.763, after hitting an intraday record high of 60.776 earlier.

data.title: Most crypto markets rose, with the AI ​​sector gaining 2.35%.
data.description: On March 30th, according to SoSoValue data, the overall cryptocurrency market showed a slight upward trend, with the AI ​​sector performing particularly well, rising 2.35% in the past 24 hours. Specifically, Siren (SIREN) rose 13.27%, Sentient (SENT) rose 12.85%, and Venice Token (VVV) rose 6.38%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 0.41%, breaking through $66,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 0.54%, breaking through the $2,000 mark. In other sectors, the Meme sector rose 0.85% in the last 24 hours, with Bonk (BONK) up 1.57%; the Layer 2 sector rose 0.48%, with Optimism (OP) up 2.14%; the CeFi sector rose 0.28%, with NEXO (NEXO) up 1.84%; and the Layer 1 sector rose 0.20%, with Zcash (ZEC) up 2.30%. Conversely, the PayFi sector fell 0.16%, with Dash (DASH) up 1.38%; and the DeFi sector fell 0.43%, with Sky (SKY) up 3.54%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiRWA, ssiAI, and ssiLayer2 indices rose 1.52%, 1.41%, and 0.77%, respectively.
